title: ipomoea bufalina
intro: Ipomoea bufalina, also known as Bufalin or Wild Potato Vine, is a type of flowering plant in the morning glory family, native to Asia and parts of the Pacific. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include cardiotonic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Ipomoea bufalina include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially aiding in the treatment of certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of the plant often include dried leaves and roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, although its use should be done under the guidance of a medical professional due to potential side effects.
